routine-status: Completed: empty header values are rejected before validation, with a regression test added. All 18 focused tests passed; no blockers or further action.
implementation-handoff: Changed `src/auth/session.ts` and `tests/auth/session.test.ts`. Expired tokens are now rejected before database writes, preventing stale-session persistence. `npm test -- session` passed all 12 tests. No migration is required; the full integration suite remains untested.
review-finding: High severity — `src/cache.ts:84` caches failed lookups for `300` seconds, turning temporary upstream errors into five-minute outages for every caller. Cache only confirmed not-found responses and add a regression test for transient `503` responses.
exact-literals: From `/Users/luiz/project`, run `rtk pytest -q` and compare the result with https://example.com/build/42. Do not use `--force` or change `$API_TIMEOUT` from `30`. If the command fails, report the exact error string `Connection refused`.
ordered-migration: First, create a verified backup at `/tmp/users.sql`. Before changing the schema, run `psql -f verify.sql` and confirm that it exits with status `0`. Then run `psql -f migration.sql`. After the migration succeeds, run `psql -f verify.sql` again. Do not remove the backup until both verification runs pass.
destructive-warning: `git reset --hard origin/main` permanently discards all uncommitted tracked changes. After unreachable objects are pruned, Git cannot recover them. Confirm the exact repository and ensure the work is committed or backed up before running the command.
ambiguous-diagnosis: Connection-pool exhaustion might cause the intermittent timeout, but the logs do not prove it; DNS latency or an upstream rate limit could produce the same symptom. We know only that failures begin after roughly `40` concurrent requests. Capture pool metrics, DNS timing, and upstream response headers before changing production limits.
